outcome: 22% auth failure rate on low-bandwidth POS terminals within 8 minutes of deployment
root_cause: 3s timeout insufficient for 43% of terminals on high-latency cellular connections under load
remediation: Rolled back to 5s. Vendor advisory confirmed minimum 4.5s under peak load.

outcome: OOM crash on Windows Server 2019 fleet, service restart loop every 4 minutes
root_cause: Windows Server 2019 kernel imposes 1800MB limit for this process class. Setting exceeded OS cap.
remediation: Reverted to 1024MB. Added OS-level memory cap validation to deployment checklist.

outcome: DDoS amplification. Bot traffic surged 8000% within 20 minutes. API gateway overwhelmed.
root_cause: Setting rate_limit to 0 disabled all rate limiting on public-facing API endpoint.
remediation: Emergency rollback. Rate limit restored to 1000/min. Added guard: rate_limit=0 is INVALID in schema.

outcome: Black Friday outage. 38% auth failure rate. POS terminals offline across 1800 stores.
root_cause: 2s timeout caused mass failures. Friday peak traffic amplified blast radius 4x. Primary engineer on PTO.
remediation: Emergency rollback. New policy: no auth.yaml changes within 72h of peak traffic windows.

outcome: False positive rate increased 340%. Legitimate transactions blocked. Customer complaints surged.
root_cause: Lowering fraud threshold too aggressively blocked legitimate transactions in new customer cohort.
remediation: Restored threshold to 0.85. Staged rollout policy added for ML model parameters.
